title: Australia Student Visa Journey
description: Step-by-step roadmap for Pakistani students applying for an Australian Student visa (Subclass 500) — from choosing your course and receiving your CoE through arriving in Australia and maintaining your visa conditions.
disclaimer: Informational guidance only. Australian visa policies and requirements can change. Always confirm current requirements with the Department of Home Affairs before taking any action.

visaOverview.title: Student Visa – Subclass 500
visaOverview.text: The Australian Student visa (Subclass 500) allows you to study a full-time registered course at an Australian education provider. You must have a Confirmation of Enrolment (CoE) from a registered provider and meet the Genuine Student requirement. Applications are lodged online through ImmiAccount on the Home Affairs website.

stages.name: Stage I: Course & Confirmation of Enrolment

steps.name: Choose a CRICOS-Registered Course and Provider
steps.description: You must study at an Australian education provider and in a course both registered on the Commonwealth Register of Institutions and Courses for Overseas Students (CRICOS).
actions: Search for CRICOS-registered providers and courses at cricos.education.gov.au
Confirm both the institution and your specific course are CRICOS-registered
Research admission requirements, course duration, and tuition costs
Apply for admission through the institution's official admissions process
Receive the official offer of enrolment from the institution
steps.output: Offer of enrolment received from a CRICOS-registered provider for a CRICOS-registered course

steps.name: Accept Offer and Receive Confirmation of Enrolment (CoE)
steps.description: After accepting the institution's offer and paying any required upfront fees, the provider issues a Confirmation of Enrolment (CoE). The CoE number is required for your visa application.
steps.info: You may receive more than one CoE if enrolled in multiple courses (e.g., packaged English + degree). Enter all CoE numbers in your visa application.
actions: Accept the offer of enrolment by the institution's deadline
Pay any upfront tuition or deposit as required
Receive your CoE and record the CoE number(s)
Verify CoE details: your name, course name, provider, course start/end dates, and level
Contact the institution immediately if any details are incorrect
steps.output: Confirmation of Enrolment (CoE) received; CoE number(s) verified and recorded

stages.name: Stage II: Genuine Student Requirement & Documents

steps.name: Understand the Genuine Student Requirement
steps.description: The Genuine Student (GS) requirement asks you to demonstrate that you are a genuine student who intends to comply with the conditions of the Student visa. The assessment is holistic.
steps.info: Read the current Genuine Student requirement guidance at immi.homeaffairs.gov.au/visas/getting-a-visa/visa-listing/student-500/genuine-student-requirement before preparing your statement. The GS questions are part of the ImmiAccount application form itself.
actions: Read the current Genuine Student requirement guidance on Home Affairs website
Prepare a truthful, specific written statement explaining your study intentions, how the course relates to your goals, and your ties to Pakistan
Gather supporting evidence reinforcing your Genuine Student statement (transcripts, prior qualifications, employment records, ties to Pakistan)
steps.output: Genuine Student statement prepared; supporting evidence assembled

steps.name: Prepare Passport and Identity Documents
steps.description: Your current Pakistani passport is required for the Student visa application. Confirm the current passport and identity document requirements on the Home Affairs website.
actions: Confirm your Pakistani passport is valid — check current requirements at immi.homeaffairs.gov.au
Scan your passport biographical page at high resolution for upload
Review whether previous passports need to be provided — check current guidance on Home Affairs
Prepare a recent passport-size photograph meeting Home Affairs specifications
Review the current identity document requirements at immi.homeaffairs.gov.au/visas/getting-a-visa/visa-listing/student-500/documents
steps.output: Valid passport confirmed; identity documents prepared and scanned for upload

steps.name: Prepare Evidence of Financial Capacity
steps.description: You must demonstrate that you have the financial capacity to pay course fees and living costs during your stay in Australia. Check the current requirements and thresholds on the Home Affairs website.
steps.warn: Do not rely on unofficial sources for required financial amounts. Check the current financial capacity requirements at immi.homeaffairs.gov.au/visas/getting-a-visa/visa-listing/student-500/financial-capacity.
actions: Review the current financial capacity requirements at immi.homeaffairs.gov.au/visas/getting-a-visa/visa-listing/student-500/financial-capacity
Obtain official bank statements covering the period specified by Home Affairs
If a sponsor is funding your studies: obtain the sponsor's financial evidence and a signed statutory declaration
If you have a scholarship: obtain the official scholarship letter
Ensure all financial documents are in English or accompanied by certified translations
steps.output: Financial capacity evidence assembled meeting current Home Affairs requirements

steps.name: Prepare Academic and English Language Evidence
steps.description: You will generally need to provide evidence of your previous academic qualifications and English language proficiency alongside your CoE. Review the current document requirements on Home Affairs.
actions: Gather academic transcripts, certificates, and diplomas for your most recent qualification
Obtain certified English translations for any documents not in English
Prepare your English language test results (IELTS, TOEFL, PTE, Cambridge, etc.) if applicable
Review the current Student visa documents checklist at immi.homeaffairs.gov.au/visas/getting-a-visa/visa-listing/student-500/documents
steps.output: Academic transcripts, qualifications, and English evidence prepared for upload

steps.name: Undergo the Required Health Examination
steps.description: Most Student visa applicants are required to undergo a health examination with an approved panel physician, booked using the Health Assessment Processing (HAP) ID generated at or after lodgement of the visa application.
steps.info: Home Affairs typically issues a HAP ID when you lodge your application — you use it to book the health examination with an approved panel physician in Pakistan. Review the current health examination guidance at immi.homeaffairs.gov.au/help-support/meeting-our-requirements/health/health-examinations.
actions: Review the current health examination requirements at immi.homeaffairs.gov.au/help-support/meeting-our-requirements/health/health-examinations
Find approved panel physicians in Pakistan using the Home Affairs panel doctor search tool
Once you have your HAP ID (from ImmiAccount after lodgement): book the health examination at an approved panel physician
Attend all required components of the examination as directed by the panel physician
Results are submitted electronically by the panel physician to Home Affairs — no separate submission required
steps.output: Health examination completed at an approved panel physician; results transmitted to Home Affairs

steps.name: Provide Biometrics (if requested)
steps.description: Home Affairs may request biometrics (fingerprints and photograph) as part of your application. If requested, you will be directed through ImmiAccount to provide them at a designated location.
steps.info: Not all applicants are asked to provide biometrics — Home Affairs will notify you through ImmiAccount. For Pakistan-based applicants, if biometrics are requested, check the instructions in your ImmiAccount notification for the current designated facility.
actions: Monitor your ImmiAccount for any biometrics request
If biometrics are requested: follow the instructions in your ImmiAccount notification carefully
Book a biometrics appointment at the facility specified in the notification
Attend with your passport and appointment confirmation
Biometrics data is transmitted directly to Home Affairs
steps.output: Biometrics provided at the designated facility (if requested by Home Affairs)

steps.name: Arrange Overseas Student Health Cover (OSHC)
steps.description: Student visa applicants must obtain Overseas Student Health Cover (OSHC) from a Home Affairs-approved provider covering the full duration of the Student visa.
steps.warn: Maintaining OSHC for your entire visa period is a visa condition. Check the current list of approved providers and requirements at immi.homeaffairs.gov.au/visas/getting-a-visa/visa-listing/student-500/overseas-student-health-cover. Do not rely on unofficial sources for OSHC costs.
actions: Review the current OSHC requirement at immi.homeaffairs.gov.au/visas/getting-a-visa/visa-listing/student-500/overseas-student-health-cover
Check the current list of Home Affairs-approved OSHC providers
Purchase OSHC covering the full period of your intended Australian stay
Record OSHC policy details: provider name, policy number, coverage dates
Confirm with your institution whether they arrange OSHC on your behalf
steps.output: OSHC purchased from an approved provider; policy details recorded

steps.name: Create or Log In to ImmiAccount
steps.description: All Australian Student visa applications are lodged online through ImmiAccount — the Department of Home Affairs online portal. Create an account if you do not already have one.
actions: Go to immi.homeaffairs.gov.au and navigate to ImmiAccount
Create an ImmiAccount using your email address if you do not have one
Log in to your ImmiAccount
Read the current how-to-apply guidance for Student visa Subclass 500 at immi.homeaffairs.gov.au/visas/getting-a-visa/visa-listing/student-500/how-to-apply
steps.output: ImmiAccount created and accessible; application guidance reviewed

steps.name: Complete the Online Application Form
steps.description: Start a new Student visa (Subclass 500) application in ImmiAccount. Complete all sections accurately, including your CoE number(s) and Genuine Student requirement responses.
actions: In ImmiAccount, select New Application and choose Student visa (Subclass 500)
Enter your CoE number(s) as requested
Complete all personal details — name, date of birth, and passport number must exactly match your documents
Respond fully and honestly to the Genuine Student requirement questions
Answer all travel history, immigration history, and character questions accurately
Review all sections carefully before proceeding
steps.output: Student visa application form fully completed in ImmiAccount

steps.name: Upload Supporting Documents
steps.description: Upload all required supporting documents to your ImmiAccount application. Documents must be clearly legible and in an accepted file format.
actions: Review the current required documents list at immi.homeaffairs.gov.au/visas/getting-a-visa/visa-listing/student-500/documents
Upload your passport biographical page scan
Upload financial capacity evidence
Upload academic transcripts and qualifications
Upload English language test results (if applicable)
Upload OSHC policy details
Ensure all files are clearly legible in an accepted format (PDF, JPEG, or as specified)
steps.output: All required documents uploaded to the ImmiAccount application

steps.name: Pay the Visa Application Charge and Lodge
steps.description: After completing the form and uploading documents, pay the visa application charge and submit the application. The charge is non-refundable even if the application is refused.
steps.warn: Check the current Student visa Subclass 500 application charge at immi.homeaffairs.gov.au/visas/getting-a-visa/fees-and-charges/current-visa-pricing/student before paying — do not rely on unofficial sources.
actions: Verify the current visa application charge at the official Home Affairs fee page
Pay the visa application charge online within ImmiAccount
Save the payment receipt
Lodge (submit) the application — record your Transaction Reference Number (TRN)
Note your HAP ID from the confirmation — you will use it to book your health examination if not already done
steps.output: Application lodged; TRN and HAP ID obtained

steps.name: Await the Decision and Respond to Any Requests
steps.description: After lodgement, Home Affairs will assess your application. Monitor your ImmiAccount and registered email for any requests for additional information.
actions: Check current Student visa processing times at immi.homeaffairs.gov.au/visas/getting-a-visa/visa-listing/student-500/processing-times
Do not make non-refundable travel arrangements until the visa is granted
Monitor your ImmiAccount and registered email for any Home Affairs requests
If additional information or documents are requested: respond completely within the stated timeframe
Confirm your health examination results have been submitted by the panel physician if applicable
steps.output: Application processing underway; all Home Affairs requests responded to

steps.name: Receive the Visa Grant Notice
steps.description: If approved, Home Affairs issues a visa grant letter to your ImmiAccount and registered email. The visa is electronic — there is no physical stamp in your passport.
steps.info: Your Student visa is an electronic visa. Verify and share your visa status using VEVO (Visa Entitlement Verification Online) at immi.homeaffairs.gov.au/visas/already-have-a-visa/check-visa-details-and-conditions/check-conditions-online. The grant letter shows your visa validity and conditions.
actions: Check ImmiAccount and your registered email for the visa grant notification
Read the visa grant letter carefully — note visa start date, end date, and conditions
Verify your visa details using VEVO
Save a copy of the grant letter
If refused: read the refusal notice for the stated reasons and review your options with reference to Home Affairs guidance
steps.output: Student visa (Subclass 500) granted; visa verified on VEVO

stages.name: Stage VI: Travel, Arrival & Visa Conditions

steps.name: Prepare for Travel and Australian Border Entry
steps.description: Once your visa is granted, plan your travel to Australia. You must arrive before your visa start date and within its validity. Carry key documents when travelling.
actions: Confirm your visa start and end dates on your grant letter and verify on VEVO
Book flights to arrive in Australia in time for your course and within your visa validity
Carry: valid Pakistani passport (linked to your visa), visa grant letter, CoE(s), OSHC policy, financial evidence, and institution accommodation details
Review any current Australian Border Force entry requirements at abf.gov.au
Keep your ImmiAccount login accessible during your stay
steps.output: Travel booked within visa validity; all required documents prepared for Australian entry

steps.name: Arrive in Australia and Clear Australian Border Force
steps.description: On arrival in Australia, present your valid passport to Australian Border Force. Your visa is electronic — no physical label is needed for entry.
actions: Present your valid Pakistani passport to Australian Border Force on arrival
Your visa is electronic — carry your grant letter in case questions arise
Obtain and complete any incoming passenger card as required by current ABF process
Proceed through customs following ABF instructions
Confirm your entry on VEVO after arrival
steps.output: Arrived in Australia; border cleared; electronic visa entry confirmed on VEVO

steps.name: Enrol and Commence Your Course
steps.description: You must enrol and actively attend your CRICOS-registered course. Your provider reports enrolment and attendance to the Australian Government via PRISMS.
actions: Attend your institution's orientation and complete enrolment by the required date
Confirm your enrolment matches your CoE course
Maintain active attendance and satisfactory academic progress per your institution's requirements
Contact your institution before making any changes to your course, provider, or deferral — understand the visa implications first
steps.output: Enrolled in CRICOS course; attendance and enrolment requirements understood

steps.name: Understand and Comply with Your Student Visa Conditions
steps.description: Your Student visa is subject to conditions that you must comply with for the full duration of your visa. Breaching conditions can result in visa cancellation.
steps.info: Review the conditions on your visa grant letter and at immi.homeaffairs.gov.au/visas/getting-a-visa/visa-listing/student-500/conditions. If unsure whether a specific activity is permitted, check with your institution's international student office or the Home Affairs website before proceeding.
actions: Read all conditions on your Student visa grant letter
Verify conditions using VEVO
Review current Student visa conditions guidance at immi.homeaffairs.gov.au/visas/getting-a-visa/visa-listing/student-500/conditions
Maintain your OSHC coverage for the full duration of your visa
If your circumstances change (course, provider, address): check Home Affairs guidance on required notifications or visa actions
steps.output: All Student visa conditions reviewed and being complied with

steps.name: Plan for Visa Extension or Post-Study Next Steps
steps.description: If your course extends beyond your current visa or you wish to continue studying, apply for a further Student visa before your current visa expires. Plan post-study pathways in advance.
actions: Track your Student visa expiry date on VEVO
If extending for further study: review the further Student visa application process at immi.homeaffairs.gov.au well before expiry
If considering post-graduation pathways (e.g., Temporary Graduate visa): check Home Affairs for eligibility requirements
If departing Australia: ensure you depart before your visa expires and in compliance with conditions
steps.output: Post-study plan confirmed; extension applied or departure planned before visa expiry

documents: Evidence of Financial Capacity (Bank Statements, Sponsorship, etc.)
English Language Test Results (IELTS, PTE, TOEFL, etc.)
